All students need to meet a minimum <strong>3.10 GPA </strong>cumulative <u>and</u> a <strong>3.20 GPA</strong> in major preparation coursework.</p><p><br></p><p>All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>Important Information:</p><p><br></p><p>Complete the courses below:</p><p>●    Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C</p><p>●    Organic Chemistry (CHE) 128A/B and 129A</p><p>●    Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 032A or Chemical Engineering (ECH) 060</p><p>●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D</p><p>●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B</p><p>●    Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C</p><p><br></p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"e0836f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>The <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Chemical Engineering</a> program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of <a href=\"https://www.abet.org/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">ABET</a> under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Chemical, Biochemical, Biomolecular, and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.</p><p><br></p><p>Chemical engineers apply the principles of chemistry and engineering to produce useful commodities, ranging from fuels to polymers. Chemical engineers are increasingly concerned with chemical and engineering processes related to the environment and food production. They work in diverse areas ranging from integrated circuits to integrated waste management. Preparation for a career in chemical engineering requires an understanding of both engineering and chemical principles to develop proficiency in conceiving, designing, and operating new processes.</p><p><br></p><p>The chemical engineering curriculum has been planned to provide a sound knowledge of engineering and chemical sciences so that you may achieve competence in addressing current and future technical problems.</p>","groupId":"e2836f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"COMPOSITION","groupId":"71289c30-1d41-4cc3-b210-01985beb98bb","position":13,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"CHEMICAL ENGINEERING & PROGRAMMING","groupId":"76123f2a-0880-4021-b05c-01985beb98bb","position":15,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"CHEMICAL ENGINEERING & PROGRAMMING","groupId":"89aac5a9-21ef-4544-8c8a-01985bec29bd","position":5,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"ENGINEERING","groupId":"2bfe00bd-e975-42ef-af1d-01985bec29be","position":17,"area":"Requirements"}]
